key_insight: A linearly implicit mass-lumped finite element projection scheme can enforce the unit-sphere constraint exactly at mesh nodes while remaining unconditionally stable and optimally accurate for harmonic map flow.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical, mesh-based PDE solver with provable energy decay and no time-step/mesh coupling, making it attractive for robust simulation of constrained geometric flows.
method: Linear predictor using a cross-product reformulation / tangent-plane equivalent scheme
Nodal projection to restore exact unit-length constraint at FE nodes
Mass-lumped discrete inner products plus tensor-product mesh energy argument for stability
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal convergence rates of O(Δt+h^2) in L∞(0,T;L2) and O(Δt+h) in L2(0,T;H1), along with numerical experiments confirming these rates and discrete energy decay.
watch_item: The unconditional discrete energy dissipation result is proved for Cartesian rectangular/cuboidal tensor-product meshes, so extension to general unstructured meshes may require additional analysis.
paper_title: Unconditional Optimal Error Estimates and Energy Stability for a Linearly Implicit Mass-Lumped Projection Finite Element Method for the Harmonic Map Flow
